items.title: CMS guidance: new limits on federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens start Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS released a State Health Official letter to prepare states to implement a statutory change limiting federal matching funds in Medicaid and CHIP for people who are not U.S. citizens/nationals or who fall outside specified noncitizen categories, starting October 1, 2026. CMS noted exceptions including continued federal matching for emergency medical conditions and that the change does not affect the option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people.

items.title: HUD revises Emergency Housing Voucher and Stability Voucher rules: removes SSN/citizenship-documentation grace period
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-05 removes the previous waiver/alternative requirement that let PHAs admit EHV/SV households without initial SSN and eligible immigration-status documentation, with up to 180 days to provide it. The notice says PHAs must follow 24 CFR 5.508 (citizenship/eligible immigration status) and 24 CFR 5.210–5.218 (SSN disclosure/verification), with a compliance deadline within 60 days of the notice’s publication.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC cash-value benefit amounts; states must offer at least one non-fresh fruit/veg form by April 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS Policy Memo 2026-2 lists the FY 2026 WIC cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts: $26/month for children, $48/month for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52/month for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. The memo also notes recent WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) beyond fresh by April 2026.

items.title: IRS: 2026 tax-year inflation adjustments include higher maximum EITC (up to $8,231 for 3+ children)
items.summary: The IRS announced inflation adjustments for tax year 2026, including that the maximum Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) will be $8,231 for taxpayers with three or more qualifying children (up from $8,046 for tax year 2025). These 2026 adjustments generally apply to returns filed in 2027.

items.title: IRS: Adoption Credit now partially refundable up to $5,000 per child (max credit $17,280) after OBBB changes
items.summary: The IRS says the Adoption Credit maximum is $17,280 per eligible child for the referenced year and that the credit is now partially refundable up to $5,000 per qualifying child after changes in the One, Big, Beautiful Bill. The IRS also notes Indian tribal governments can determine whether a child has special needs for purposes of the credit.

items.title: Education Dept.: apply by April 23, 2026 for Title III designation and waiver of non-federal share for FWS/FSEOG
items.summary: Federal Student Aid posted an electronic announcement (CB-26-06) reminding institutions to apply by April 23, 2026 for designation as a Title III institution and a waiver of the non-federal share requirement for Federal Work-Study (FWS) and Federal Supplemental Educational Opportunity Grant (FSEOG). The announcement is aimed at participating schools and financial aid administrators.
items.source: Federal Student Aid (U.S. Department of Education)

items.title: USDA WIC Q&A: states must implement WIC food package updates within 24 months (with specific earlier deadlines for some provisions)
items.summary: USDA’s WIC food package Q&A explains state agencies must implement the final WIC food package changes within 24 months of publication, with some exceptions. USDA notes that increased cash-value benefit (CVV) amounts must be implemented no later than 60 days from the final rule’s publication.

items.title: USDA approves additional state SNAP waivers restricting certain foods starting in 2026
items.summary: USDA announced approval of six new state SNAP food-choice waivers (Hawai‘i, Missouri, North Dakota, South Carolina, Virginia, Tennessee) under its Make America Healthy Again initiative. USDA said the waivers will amend the statutory definition of “food for purchase” in SNAP beginning in 2026, and noted earlier approvals brought the total to 12 states with approved waivers.

items.title: CMS bulletin: enforcement discretion delays certain Medicaid↔CHIP transition notice/account-transfer requirements until June 3, 2026
items.summary: CMS guidance says it will use enforcement discretion and not require states to comply with two coverage-transition requirements until June 3, 2026: sending combined notices when a child transitions between Medicaid and separate CHIP, and transferring accounts to the Marketplace for people procedurally disenrolled from Medicaid/CHIP. The bulletin explains the underlying seamless-transition requirement began June 3, 2024.
items.source: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (via SHVS)

items.title: KFF: Medicaid policy changes in 2026 include provider-tax limits and early state moves on work requirements
items.summary: KFF reports the 2025 reconciliation law is estimated to increase the number of uninsured people by 7.5 million in 2034, including 5.3 million tied to new Medicaid work requirements starting January 1, 2027. KFF also notes some states may need to revise certain provider taxes as early as April 1, 2026 due to new limits, and that Nebraska announced it plans to enforce federal work requirements early starting May 1, 2026.

items.title: SSA: SSI federal maximum payment amounts for 2026 rise with 2.8% COLA (up to $994/month for an individual)
items.summary: SSA’s COLA table shows the monthly maximum federal SSI payment amounts for 2026 are $994 for an eligible individual, $1,491 for an eligible individual with an eligible spouse, and $498 for an essential person. SSA notes these amounts reflect a 2.8% COLA effective January 2026.
